violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 126
; VP2 continued: can vacuousCount undercount?
; Would require isVacuous to return false for truly vacuous blocks

; Truly vacuous: no declarations, no assertions
(declare-const decls Int)
(declare-const defFuns Int)
(declare-const asserts Int)

; True vacuous: no vars declared, no assertions
(assert (= decls 0))
(assert (= defFuns 0))
(assert (= asserts 0))

; isVacuous returns false (line 50: "if declares.length === 0 && defFuns.length === 0) return false")
; So truly empty blocks are NOT counted as vacuous

; This is a classification issue:
; isVacuous(true) = true only when there are declarations but no asserts
; isVacuous(false) for completely empty blocks

; The log says "vacuous blocks filtered" - but empty blocks aren't counted
(assert (and (= decls 0) (= asserts 0)))
(check-sat)
